news.title: Practical weight, explained — plus a few more DevSecOps certs
news.body: Got some really good feedback on the recent map update — especially around the practical weight scoring. As a few of you pointed out, I never actually shared how that number is decided. So here it is, the 5-tier scale: 100% — Entirely practical · 70% — Majority practical + report · 50% — Hybrid MCQ + lab · 25% — MCQ + optional lab · 0% — MCQ only. This now lives behind a small ? tooltip next to the practical bar in every cert profile and next to the 'Practical' color-by selector on the map, so you can pull it up any time. Also slipped in a few more certs from the Practical DevSecOps catalog (CCSE Container, CCNSE Cloud-Native, CSC Security Champion) and refreshed the existing PDSO entries with better scoring and cross-domain placement. Keep the feedback coming — this is exactly how the project gets better.

news.title: Big update — Map view just landed (v1, feedback wanted!)
news.body: Been planning this one for a while. EBCertMap started as Paul Jerimy's roadmap done as a searchable database — but the original's strength is its visual at-a-glance table. So I built one. Toggle between ⊞ Grid and ⬡ Map at the top of the Certs view to see all 426 certs as a domain × level matrix. Click any pill for the full profile. Pick a single domain to switch to a leaderboard-per-level view (best-first by overall score) — way more useful when you're shopping inside one specialization. Color the pills by Domain, Employer Recognition, Practical Weight, or Cost to scan the catalog from different angles. Mobile gets a vertical leaderboard layout. This is v1 — I'd really like feedback on this one. What's missing? What's confusing? What would make it more useful for how you actually browse?

news.title: New role + budget-friendly paths
news.body: Added a few new role paths around the recent boutique certs. Most notable: split mobile security into two roles instead of cramming everything under Mobile Pentester — kept that role focused on app testing (CMSE/CISE/CASE), and created a dedicated Mobile Security Researcher role for the deeper kernel/internals/ARM exploitation track (CISR → CASR → OAAE → OMSE). Two roles, clearer career story. Also added a budget CTI path (arcX FTIA → PTIA → MCTIA) at $630 instead of $999+ for GCTI, a malware mobile track, and AIRTP+ as the entry into AI red teaming. Plus a quiet roles.json cleanup so the metadata actually matches the path files.

news.title: New boutique certs added — OMSE, CMSE and more
news.body: Found and added a bunch of lesser-known but genuinely valuable certs that don't get enough attention — including 8kSec's mobile security stack (OMSE, CMSE, OAAE, CISE, CASE, CISR, CASR, CAMRE, CIMRE, CMMRE, CAISR), Mandiant's MCTIA, arcX FTIA/PTIA, and Learn Prompting's AIRTP+. These are hands-on, practical-format credentials that build real skill rather than just exam prep — worth a look if you're going deep on mobile security, AI red teaming, or threat intelligence.

news.title: Better platform support & Google indexing
news.body: Added improved support for different platforms (LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and other social previews now show proper images and descriptions) and fixed Google search indexing — so EBCertMap should now be easier to find when you search for cybersecurity certs. If you stumbled here through a search engine, welcome!

news.title: Huge update — 40+ new certs, 6 new issuers
news.body: Thanks to all the feedback I got from all of you — this update is a big one. More than 40 new certificates across 6 new issuers have been added (see the Changelog for specifics). Cross-domain certs have also been fixed and fact-checked, so certs that belong to multiple domains now correctly show up everywhere they should. With all these new certs we now have a lot more possible paths for the roles view — expect a roles update soon!

news.title: Posted on r/cybersecurity — feedback welcome
news.body: EBCertMap is now live on Reddit for community review. If you have feedback on cert scores, missing certs, or domain coverage — drop it in the thread. Every data point helps. 🔗 reddit.com/r/cybersecurity/comments/1srpwfs

news.title: Added phone support layout for the certmap
news.body: EBCertMap is now fully usable on mobile. The layout adapts at 768px: domain pills scroll horizontally in the Certs view, the Skills panel opens as an overlay, and the Roles view switches to a drill-down flow (roles → paths → cert profile). Desktop experience is unchanged.

news.title: What's coming next
news.body: Planning to add course recommendations alongside each cert path, a dedicated study tracker, and a comparison tool so you can put two certs side by side. Also looking at getting a proper domain for this.

changelog.title: DevSecOps Cert Refresh, Duplicate Merge & Practical-Weight Legend
items: Added 3 new Practical DevSecOps certs: CCSE (Container Security Expert), CCNSE (Cloud-Native Security Expert), CSC (Security Champion)
Refreshed 6 existing PDSO certs with revised scoring and cross-domain placement: CDP, CDE, CSSE now span DefSec/SOC + AppSec; CTMP moved to Security Architecture; CAISP and CASP score updates
Refreshed CKS and KCSA (CNCF) entries with current 2026 exam details and the Kubestronaut program reference

Merged 3 long-standing duplicate cert entries: AAIA (was both 'aaia' and 'isaca-aaia'), AIGP (split across data_privacy and ai_security), CISSP (split across security_architecture and iam) — kept the richer copy of each, expanded domain arrays where needed
Added issuer-initial tags to map pills for genuine vendor-collision acronyms (CCSE × 3, CPIA × 2) — pills now show '·CP', '·CWL', '·PDSO', '·CREST' so you can tell them apart at a glance

Added a '?' popover next to the practical-weight bar in the cert profile and next to the active 'Practical' color-by selector on the map — shows the 5-tier scoring scale (100% Entirely practical → 0% MCQ only)

changelog.title: Map View — Visual Domain × Level Roadmap
items: Added ⊞ Grid / ⬡ Map toggle to the Certs view — Map shows all 426 certs as a visual domain × level matrix inspired by Paul Jerimy's roadmap
All-domains map: 15 columns × 4 levels (Elite → Beginner), cross-domain certs appear in every relevant column
Single-domain map: switches to a leaderboard layout — one wide row per level, certs sorted best-first by overall score with score badges baked into each pill
Color pills by 4 dimensions — Domain, Employer Recognition, Practical Weight, or Cost — clickable blocks in the left legend
Mobile map view — same leaderboard layout in a vertical stack with a horizontal color-by chip strip; the toggle works on phones too
Empty cells get a subtle dotted background so the structure stays readable
Map respects all active filters (search, level, issuer, cost, DoD, ≥50% practical) — switching modes preserves what you were looking at
Profile pane shrinks to 320px in map mode to give the grid more breathing room
Auto-loads all 15 domain JSONs the first time you enter map mode so the grid isn't sparse
Replaced the default Vite/React favicon with the EB logo — same green→cyan gradient and bold 'EB' as the in-app header so the browser tab matches the brand

changelog.title: Minor UI Fixes
items: Fixed mobile role view — tapping the same role after going back to the role list now re-opens it (previously you had to tap a different role first)
Changed default Certs view domain from 'Offensive' to 'All Domains' so visitors see the full catalog on first load
Added missing 'Level' and 'Issuer' sort options to the mobile sort dropdown — mobile now matches desktop's full 6-option sort menu

changelog.title: Cloudflare Security Hardening
items: Enabled 'Always Use HTTPS' — all HTTP requests now 301 redirect to HTTPS
Raised minimum TLS version from 1.0 to 1.2 — drops deprecated, vulnerable TLS versions (POODLE, BEAST exposure surface)
Enabled HSTS with 6-month max-age — browsers now remember to always use HTTPS for ebcertmap.com
Left HSTS subdomain inclusion and preload list off as safe defaults — can be revisited if subdomains are added later
Enabled X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header — prevents browsers from MIME-type guessing on responses

changelog.title: New Role, New Paths & Roles Cleanup
items: Added new role: Mobile Security Researcher — for practitioners targeting platform vulnerability research (CISR → CASR → OAAE → OMSE)
Added cti_budget path for CTI Analyst — arcX FTIA → arcX PTIA → MCTIA ($630, 5-8 months) — accessible CREST-aligned route into proctored CTI
Added 8kSec Practitioner Track for Mobile Pentester — CASE → CISE → CMSE for app-testing-focused mobile security
Added malware_mobile track for Malware Analyst — CAMRE → CIMRE → CMMRE for mobile malware reverse engineering
Added AIRTP+ as the entry step in the AI Red Team path
Refocused Mobile Pentester role on app testing only — kernel/internals research now lives in the dedicated Mobile Security Researcher role to avoid overlap
Cleaned up roles.json — fixed long-standing path_count and path_ids drift across 13 roles so metadata now matches the actual path files

changelog.title: Boutique Mobile, AI & CTI Certs — 15 New Entries
items: Added 11 8kSec mobile/AI security certs: OMSE, CMSE, OAAE, CISE, CASE, CISR, CASR, CAMRE, CIMRE, CMMRE, CAISR — covering iOS/Android app security, ARM exploitation, mobile malware reverse engineering, and AI security research
Added Mandiant MCTIA — Mandiant Academy's standalone, Kryterion-proctored CTI analyst certification (NIST/NICE AN-ASA-001 aligned)
Added arcX FTIA (free Foundation) and arcX PTIA (Practitioner, CREST CPTIA prep) — CREST-accredited training credentials for the threat intelligence track
Added Learn Prompting AIRTP+ — practical AI red teaming professional cert (HackAPrompt-affiliated, 24+ hour take-home assessment)
Updated cert count to 425+ across all metadata, sitemap, and structured data
Verified zero duplicate IDs across all 15 cert domain files
Fixed issuer dropdown alphabetical sort — case-insensitive ordering so vendors like 'arcX' appear in the right place instead of after 'Zscaler'

changelog.title: SEO & Search Indexing Improvements
items: Added canonical URL tag to fix Google Search Console 'Duplicate without user-selected canonical' warning
Added JSON-LD structured data (WebApplication schema) for richer search results
Added Open Graph image (og-image.png) with proper dimensions for LinkedIn and Facebook previews
Upgraded Twitter card to summary_large_image with dedicated image
Added robots.txt referencing the sitemap
Expanded meta keywords to cover more search intents (CRTO, penetration testing, SOC analyst, GRC, DoD 8140, etc.)
Updated sitemap lastmod date and cert count to 410+

changelog.title: New Roles, Threat Intel Certs & 13Cubed Restore
items: Added 4 new roles with cert paths: Offensive Developer, OSINT Analyst, Purple Team Analyst, Payment Security Specialist
Added Threat Intelligence Analyst cert path (CTI Standard and CTI Government tracks)
Added 11 threat intelligence certs: GCTI, CTIA, CD-CTIA, MISP Analyst, RF Certified, Anomali Certified, OPSWAT CTI, CREST CIP, GSOA, OSIP, GOSI

Restored 4 13Cubed certs accidentally dropped in the previous update: IWE (Investigating Windows Endpoints), IWM (Investigating Windows Memory), ILD (Investigating Linux Devices), IME (Investigating macOS Endpoints)
Updated DFIR Practical Skills Track — IWE and IWM now load correctly in the path sequence

CyberWarFare Labs — CRTA, CRTS, CRT-ID, AD-RTS, CELMS, CESC-AS, CWI-RTO, CSCO, CEDP, CRT-COI, CPTA, COPO, CPIA, CCSE, Web-RTA, API-RTA, MCRTA, AzRTS, CARTS, CGRTS, CHMRTS, DO-RTA, K8s-RTA, MCBTA

TryHackMe — SAL1, PT1 | Red Hat — EX415 | PeopleCert/Axelos — RESILIA Foundation, RESILIA Practitioner, ITIL4-ISM | PCI SSC — PCIP | IntelTechniques — OSIP

changelog.title: Mobile Layout Support

changelog.title: Initial Project Launch
items: Built core cert browser across 15 domains with 400+ certs
Skills mapping view — select skills to find matching certs
Role-based career path explorer with 40+ roles and 125+ cert path steps
All-domains view with expandable sidebar grouped by domain then level
Dynamic cert counts, live filtering, sort, and search
